original: Grade inflation — the gradual increase in average GPAs (grade-point averages) over the past few decades — is often considered a product of a consumer era in higher education, in which students are treated like customers to be pleased.
translation_literal: 成绩膨胀，即过去几十年中平均 GPA 的逐渐上升，常被认为是高等教育消费者时代的产物，在这个时代里，学生被当作需要取悦的顾客来对待。

chunks.english: the gradual increase in average GPAs (grade-point averages) over the past few decades
chunks.chinese: 过去几十年中平均 GPA（平均绩点）的逐渐上升
chunks.relation: 破折号中解释 Grade inflation

chunks.english: a product of a consumer era in higher education
chunks.chinese: 高等教育消费者时代的产物
chunks.relation: 补充说明 Grade inflation 被认为是什么

chunks.english: in which students are treated like customers to be pleased
chunks.chinese: 在这个时代中学生被当作需要取悦的顾客来对待

chunks.relation: in which 从句内部主语
in which 从句内部谓语
补充说明 students 被如何对待

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语 — 解释 — 谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 破折号中解释 Grade inflation 的含义，阅读时可先抓主干。

original: But another, related force — a policy often buried deep in course catalogs called “grade forgiveness” — is helping raise GPAs.
translation_literal: 但是另一种相关力量，即一种常被深埋在课程目录中、被称为“成绩宽恕”的政策，正在帮助提高 GPA。

chunks.english: a policy often buried deep in course catalogs called “grade forgiveness”
chunks.chinese: 一种常被深埋在课程目录中、被称为“成绩宽恕”的政策
chunks.relation: 破折号中解释 another force

chunks.english: often buried deep in course catalogs
chunks.chinese: 常被深埋在课程目录中的

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语 — 同位解释 — 谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: 破折号中 policy... 解释 another force，主干是 force is helping raise GPAs。

key_phrases.meaning: 成绩宽恕；低分重修替换政策

original: Grade forgiveness allows students to retake a course in which they received a low grade, and the most recent grade or the highest grade is the only one that counts in calculating a student’s overall GPA.
translation_literal: 成绩宽恕允许学生重修一门他们曾获得低分的课程，并且最近一次成绩或最高成绩是计算学生总 GPA 时唯一计入的成绩。

chunks.english: Grade forgiveness allows students to retake a course in which they received a low grade
chunks.chinese: 成绩宽恕允许学生重修一门他们曾获得低分的课程

chunks.english: to retake a course in which they received a low grade
chunks.chinese: 重修一门他们曾获得低分的课程
chunks.relation: 补充说明 students 做什么

chunks.english: in which they received a low grade
chunks.chinese: 他们在其中曾获得低分的

chunks.relation: in which 从句内部主语
in which 从句内部谓语

chunks.english: and the most recent grade or the highest grade is the only one that counts in calculating a student’s overall GPA
chunks.chinese: 并且最近一次成绩或最高成绩是计算学生总 GPA 时唯一计入的成绩

chunks.english: the most recent grade or the highest grade
chunks.chinese: 最近一次成绩或最高成绩

chunks.english: the only one that counts in calculating a student’s overall GPA
chunks.chinese: 计算学生总 GPA 时唯一计入的成绩

chunks.english: that counts in calculating a student’s overall GPA
chunks.chinese: 在计算学生总 GPA 时计入的

chunks.english: in calculating a student’s overall GPA
chunks.chinese: 在计算学生总 GPA 时

sentence_patterns.explanation: and 连接两个完整分句，分别说明重修机会和成绩计算规则。

original: The use of this little-known practice has accelerated in recent years, as colleges continue to do their utmost to keep students in school (and paying tuition) and improve their graduation rates.
translation_literal: 这种鲜为人知做法的使用近年来已经加速，因为大学继续尽最大努力让学生留在学校（并缴纳学费）并提高毕业率。

chunks.english: The use of this little-known practice
chunks.chinese: 这种鲜为人知做法的使用

chunks.english: as colleges continue to do their utmost to keep students in school (and paying tuition) and improve their graduation rates
chunks.chinese: 因为大学继续尽最大努力让学生留在学校并提高毕业率

chunks.english: to keep students in school (and paying tuition) and improve their graduation rates
chunks.chinese: 为了让学生留在学校并提高毕业率
chunks.relation: 说明 do their utmost 的目的

chunks.english: to keep students in school (and paying tuition)
chunks.chinese: 让学生留在学校（并缴纳学费）

sentence_patterns.explanation: 两个不定式动作并列说明 colleges do their utmost 的目的。

original: When this practice first started decades ago, it was usually limited to freshmen, to give them a second chance to take a class in their first year if they struggled in their transition to college-level courses.
translation_literal: 当这种做法几十年前刚开始时，它通常仅限于大一新生，为的是在他们向大学水平课程过渡遇到困难时，给他们在第一年重修一门课的第二次机会。

chunks.english: When this practice first started decades ago
chunks.chinese: 当这种做法几十年前刚开始时
chunks.relation: 说明 was limited 的时间
作 limited to 的宾语

chunks.english: to give them a second chance to take a class in their first year if they struggled in their transition to college-level courses
chunks.chinese: 为了给他们在第一年重修课程的第二次机会，如果他们在向大学课程过渡时遇到困难
chunks.relation: 说明 limited to freshmen 的目的
作 give 的间接宾语

chunks.english: a second chance to take a class in their first year
chunks.chinese: 在第一年修一门课的第二次机会
chunks.relation: 作 give 的直接宾语

chunks.english: to take a class in their first year
if they struggled in their transition to college-level courses
chunks.chinese: 如果他们在向大学水平课程过渡中遇到困难
chunks.relation: 说明 give chance 的条件

chunks.english: in their transition to college-level courses
chunks.chinese: 在他们向大学水平课程的过渡中

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.explanation: 目的状语较长，chance 后又接不定式和 if 条件从句，理解时要分层。

original: But now most colleges, save for many selective campuses, allow all undergraduates, and even graduate students, to get their low grades forgiven.
translation_literal: 但是现在，大多数大学，除了许多选择性很强的校园之外，允许所有本科生，甚至研究生，让他们的低分得到宽恕。

chunks.chinese: 除了许多选择性很强的校园之外
chunks.relation: 限制 most colleges 的范围

chunks.english: all undergraduates, and even graduate students
chunks.chinese: 所有本科生，甚至研究生

chunks.english: to get their low grades forgiven
chunks.chinese: 让他们的低分得到宽恕
chunks.relation: 补充说明学生被允许做什么
补充说明 grades 的结果状态

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主语, save for..., 谓语
sentence_patterns.explanation: save for... 插在主语后，说明例外范围。

key_words.meaning: 选择性强的；录取严格的

original: College officials also tend to emphasize that the goal of grade forgiveness is less about the grade itself and more about encouraging students to retake courses critical to their degree program and graduation without incurring a big penalty.
translation_literal: 大学官员也倾向于强调，成绩宽恕的目标与其说是成绩本身，不如说是鼓励学生重修对其学位项目和毕业至关重要的课程，而不招致大的惩罚。

chunks.english: that the goal of grade forgiveness is less about the grade itself and more about encouraging students to retake courses critical to their degree program and graduation without incurring a big penalty
chunks.chinese: 成绩宽恕的目标与其说是成绩本身，不如说是鼓励学生重修关键课程且不招致大惩罚

chunks.english: less about the grade itself and more about encouraging students to retake courses critical to their degree program and graduation
chunks.chinese: 较少关于成绩本身，更多关于鼓励学生重修对学位和毕业关键的课程

chunks.english: more about encouraging students to retake courses critical to their degree program and graduation
chunks.chinese: 更多关于鼓励学生重修对学位项目和毕业关键的课程

chunks.english: critical to their degree program and graduation
chunks.chinese: 对他们的学位项目和毕业至关重要的

chunks.relation: 说明 retake courses 的伴随限制

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: less about A and more about B
sentence_patterns.explanation: less...and more... 形成对比，真正重点在 encouraging students to retake courses。

original: “Ultimately,” said Jack Miner, Ohio State University’s registrar, “we see students achieve more success because they retake a course and do better in subsequent courses or master the content that allows them to graduate on time.”
translation_literal: 俄亥俄州立大学注册主任杰克·迈纳说：“最终，我们看到学生取得更多成功，因为他们重修一门课程，并在后续课程中表现更好，或者掌握了使他们能够按时毕业的内容。”

chunks.english: said Jack Miner, Ohio State University’s registrar
chunks.chinese: 俄亥俄州立大学注册主任杰克·迈纳说
俄亥俄州立大学注册主任
chunks.relation: 解释 Jack Miner 的身份
补充说明 students 的行为

chunks.english: because they retake a course and do better in subsequent courses or master the content that allows them to graduate on time
chunks.chinese: 因为他们重修一门课程并在后续课程中表现更好，或者掌握使他们按时毕业的内容
chunks.relation: 说明 achieve success 的原因
说明 do better 的范围

chunks.english: the content that allows them to graduate on time
chunks.chinese: 使他们能够按时毕业的内容

chunks.english: that allows them to graduate on time
chunks.chinese: 使他们能够按时毕业的

chunks.relation: 补充说明 them 做什么

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 引语中插入 said + 人名身份
sentence_patterns.explanation: said Jack Miner... 插入在引语开头后，说明说话者及身份。
because 从句内部有三个并列动作，解释学生成功的原因。

key_words.meaning: 注册主任；教务注册负责人

original: That said, there is a way in which grade forgiveness satisfies colleges’ own needs as well.
translation_literal: 话虽如此，存在一种方式，在这种方式中成绩宽恕也满足了大学自身的需要。

chunks.relation: there be 结构真实主语

chunks.english: in which grade forgiveness satisfies colleges’ own needs as well
chunks.chinese: 在这种方式中成绩宽恕也满足了大学自己的需要

chunks.relation: in which 从句内部主语
in which 从句内部谓语

sentence_patterns.explanation: That said 承接上文并转折，提示下面补充另一面。

original: For public institutions, state funds are sometimes tied partly to their success on metrics such as graduation rates and student retention — so better grades can, by boosting figures like those, mean more money.
translation_literal: 对于公立机构来说，州资金有时部分地与它们在毕业率和学生留存率等指标上的成功挂钩，所以更好的成绩可以通过提高那些数字而意味着更多资金。

chunks.relation: 说明 are tied 的适用对象

chunks.english: their success on metrics such as graduation rates and student retention
chunks.chinese: 它们在毕业率和学生留存率等指标上的成功
chunks.relation: 作 tied to 的宾语

chunks.english: such as graduation rates and student retention
chunks.chinese: 例如毕业率和学生留存率

chunks.english: so better grades can, by boosting figures like those, mean more money
chunks.chinese: 所以更好的成绩可以通过提高那些数字意味着更多资金

chunks.relation: 插入说明 mean more money 的方式

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: 主句 — so 结果分句
sentence_patterns.explanation: 破折号后的 so 分句说明前面资金挂钩机制导致的结果。
by boosting figures like those 插在情态动词和谓语之间，说明方式。

original: And anything that raises GPAs will likely make students — who, at the end of the day, are paying the bill — feel they’ve gotten a better value for their tuition dollars, which is another big concern for colleges.
translation_literal: 并且任何提高 GPA 的东西都很可能使学生觉得他们的学费花得更值，而这些学生归根到底是在付账；这也是大学的另一个重大关切。

chunks.chinese: 任何提高 GPA 的东西

chunks.english: who, at the end of the day, are paying the bill
at the end of the day
feel they’ve gotten a better value for their tuition dollars
chunks.chinese: 觉得他们的学费花得更值
chunks.relation: 补充说明 students 的感受

chunks.english: they’ve gotten a better value for their tuition dollars
chunks.chinese: 他们已经为学费美元获得更好的价值

chunks.english: a better value for their tuition dollars
which is another big concern for colleges
chunks.chinese: 这也是大学的另一个重大关切
chunks.relation: 补充说明前面整个结果

chunks.chinese: 大学的另一个重大关切

sentence_patterns.explanation: 破折号中定语从句插入在宾语 students 后，主干是 anything will make students feel。

sentence_patterns.pattern_name: 分裂结构 / 间隔结构
sentence_patterns.pattern_form: make + 宾语 + feel + 宾语从句
sentence_patterns.explanation: make 的宾语和宾补之间插入较长定语从句，容易误断主干。

original: Indeed, grade forgiveness is just another way that universities are responding to consumers’ expectations for higher education.
translation_literal: 事实上，成绩宽恕只是大学回应消费者对高等教育期待的另一种方式。

chunks.english: just another way that universities are responding to consumers’ expectations for higher education
chunks.chinese: 只是大学回应消费者对高等教育期待的另一种方式

chunks.english: that universities are responding to consumers’ expectations for higher education
chunks.chinese: 大学回应消费者对高等教育期待的
消费者对高等教育的期待
chunks.relation: 作 responding to 的宾语

original: Since students and parents expect a college degree to lead to a job, it is in the best interest of a school to turn out graduates who are as qualified as possible — or at least appear to be.
translation_literal: 既然学生和家长期望大学学位通向一份工作，那么培养尽可能合格的毕业生，或者至少看起来合格的毕业生，符合学校的最大利益。

chunks.english: Since students and parents expect a college degree to lead to a job
chunks.chinese: 既然学生和家长期望大学学位通向工作

chunks.relation: 补充说明 degree 的预期结果

chunks.english: in the best interest of a school
to turn out graduates who are as qualified as possible — or at least appear to be
chunks.chinese: 培养尽可能合格，或至少看起来合格的毕业生

chunks.english: graduates who are as qualified as possible
chunks.relation: 作 turn out 的宾语

chunks.english: who are as qualified as possible
or at least appear to be
chunks.chinese: 或者至少看起来是如此
chunks.relation: 省略 qualified，补充 graduates 至少看起来合格

sentence_patterns.pattern_form: it is in the best interest... to do...
sentence_patterns.explanation: it 是形式主语，真正主语是不定式 to turn out graduates。
be 后省略 qualified，承接前文 as qualified as possible。

original: On this, students’ and colleges’ incentives seem to be aligned.
translation_literal: 在这一点上，学生和大学的激励似乎是一致的。

chunks.relation: 作 seem to be 的表语
